## [00:00 - 01:33]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer. And today we're going to talk about how you should keep it simple. Let's go. When it comes to meta-advertising, less is more. Stop overthinking optimization. What is the action you want? Optimize for that. Stop wasting time with targeting. Because of the various audience expansion products, meta wants you to go broad. In most cases, you should. Stop messing with placements. You think some placements won't work. So what? The algorithm will adjust. Especially if you optimize for a conversion, just use advantage placements. You usually don't need to create a bunch of ad variations. Use multiple text options and standard enhancements instead. Otherwise, you're just watering down the results. You rarely need all of those ad sets. You are probably creating them to test a bunch of different audiences. This might result in audience fragmentation. Just stick with the one ad set. Create multiple campaigns to satisfy your different business goals. Top of the funnel, lead building, and purchases. You really shouldn't need much more than that. There are always going to be exceptions, especially with bigger budgets. But stop overthinking this stuff. So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok, do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate, and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
